factors.summary: Key sensitivities include foreign exchange impacts and raw material costs.

evidence.text: Foreign currency impacts (net of hedging) decreased operating income and pre-tax earnings by approximately $76 million year-on-year for the first quarter of 2023, primarily resulting from the strength of the U.S. dollar. These estimates include: (a) the effects of year-on-year changes in exchange rates on translating current period functional currency profits into U.S. dollars and on current period non-functional currency denominated purchases or transfers of goods between 3M operations, and (b) year-on-year changes in transaction gains and losses, including derivative instruments designed to reduce foreign currency exchange rate risks.
evidence.subsection: Managing currency risks:

factors.summary: Unit volumes are declining across multiple business segments, regions, and markets, driven by factors such as lower sales volumes, manufacturing and supply chain disruptions, geopolitical impacts in Europe, and COVID-related issues in China. factors.summary: The company is experiencing a deceleration in organic revenue growth across multiple segments. This decline is attributed to various factors including lower sales volumes, particularly in electronics and consumer retail, manufacturing and supply chain challenges, impacts from COVID-19 in China, geopolitical issues in Europe, and the exit from Russia. Additionally, consumer electronics end-market weakness and inventory reductions by tiers and original equipment manufacturers, especially in the automotive and aerospace sectors, are contributing to the growth deceleration.

evidence.text: Total organic growth/productivity and other resulted in a net year-on-year decline of $0.17 per share which was impacted by the following: Lower sales volumes (particularly electronics/consumer retail); manufacturing/supply chain headwinds; China (COVID-related); and Europe geopolitical impacts.
evidence.subsection: Earnings per share attributable to 3M common shareholders – diluted:

evidence.text: Organic sales (6.0) % • Sales increased in automotive aftermarket, electrical markets, and abrasives and decreased in personal safety, industrial adhesives and tapes, closure and masking systems and in roofing granules. • Growth was held back by the disposable respirator sales decline within personal safety along with the exit of Russia (which, together negatively impacted year-on-year first quarter organic growth by 9.9 percentage points); declines within industrial adhesives and tapes due to consumer electronics softness, closure and masking systems was down as consumers pulled back on discretionary spending impacting e-commerce shipments.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended March 31, (Percent of pre-tax income) 2023 2022 Effective tax rate 17.7   % 18.8  %

evidence.text: Organic sales (8.0) % Divestitures (1.0) Translation (3.4) Total sales change (12.4) % On an organic sales basis: • Sales increased in automotive and aerospace, and advanced materials and decreased in electronics, transportation safety and commercial solutions. • Growth continued to be held back by significant consumer electronics end-market weakness along with tiers and original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) aggressively reducing inventories particularly for smartphones, tablets and TVs.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended March 31, (Percent of pre-tax income) 2023 2022 Effective tax rate 17.7   % 18.8  %

evidence.text: Organic sales (6.8) % Divestitures (0.3) Translation (1.9) Total sales change (9.0) % On an organic sales basis: • Sales increased in stationery and office, and decreased in home improvement and home health and auto care. • Growth was negatively impacted as consumers have shifted their spending patterns to more non-discretionary items and retailers have aggressively reduced their inventory levels.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended March 31, (Percent of pre-tax income) 2023 2022 Effective tax rate 17.7   % 18.8  %

sentiment.label: very_negative
